identity.name: Insight
identity.definition: An Insight is an AI-generated recommendation derived from the Skills Grid's proficiency data, scoped to the currently visible domain, that helps teachers translate raw proficiency patterns into specific instructional actions.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Insight appears in the Skills Grid side panel

examples.title: Classwide Learning Need: Number Sense
examples.body: Most students in this class are showing limited proficiency in Number Sense & Operations. Consider whole-class re-teaching of foundational concepts before moving on.

examples.title: Suggested Grouping: Geometry Foundations
examples.body: These 5 students share similar proficiency gaps in Geometry & Measurement. Group them for targeted practice on shape properties and area calculations.

attributes.name: Title
attributes.description: Concise headline summarizing the insight recommendation.

attributes.name: Insight Type
attributes.description: Category of insight: classwide_learning_need or student_grouping_suggestion.

attributes.name: Referenced Students
attributes.description: Students relevant to this insight recommendation.

attributes.name: Referenced Standard/Domain
attributes.description: The standard or domain this insight pertains to.

attributes.name: Feedback Status
attributes.description: Teacher feedback on insight quality: none, helpful, or not_helpful.

attributes.name: Title
attributes.description: Concise headline summarizing the insight recommendation.

attributes.name: Body/Description
attributes.description: Full narrative explanation of the insight with supporting data.

attributes.name: Insight Type
attributes.description: Category of insight: classwide_learning_need or student_grouping_suggestion.

attributes.name: Insight ID
attributes.description: Unique system identifier for this insight instance.

attributes.name: Referenced Students
attributes.description: Students relevant to this insight recommendation.

attributes.name: Referenced Standard/Domain
attributes.description: The standard or domain this insight pertains to.

attributes.name: Feedback Status
attributes.description: Teacher feedback on insight quality: none, helpful, or not_helpful.

attributes.name: Dismissed
attributes.description: Whether the teacher has dismissed this insight from view.

stories.title: View Insights
stories.benefit: I can quickly understand AI-identified proficiency patterns and get actionable recommendations for my class.
stories.whenClause: I open the Skills Grid and navigate to a domain,
stories.thenClause: I see AI-generated Insights in the side panel that highlight classwide learning needs and student grouping suggestions for the currently visible domain.

stories.title: Act on Grouping Suggestion
stories.benefit: I can form targeted small groups based on shared proficiency gaps without manually analyzing each student's data.
stories.whenClause: I review a student grouping suggestion Insight,
stories.thenClause: I see which students share similar gaps, the relevant standard, and can use this information to create focused instructional groups.

stories.title: Provide Feedback on Insight
stories.benefit: the AI engine can improve future recommendations based on what teachers find useful.
stories.whenClause: I mark an Insight as helpful or not helpful,
stories.thenClause: my feedback is recorded and used to refine the relevance and quality of future insight generation.

businessRules.title: Domain Scoping
businessRules.description: Insights are scoped to the currently visible domain in the Skills Grid. Changing the domain view refreshes the insight panel with domain-relevant recommendations.

businessRules.title: Generation Trigger
businessRules.description: Insights are generated by the AI engine when a teacher opens or navigates within the Skills Grid. Generation is automatic and does not require explicit user action.

businessRules.title: Insight Types
businessRules.description: Only two insight types exist: classwide_learning_need (patterns across the whole class) and student_grouping_suggestion (clusters of students with similar gaps).

businessRules.title: Student References
businessRules.description: Each Insight references zero or more Students. Classwide insights may reference most or all students; grouping suggestions reference the specific cluster.

businessRules.title: Standard References
businessRules.description: Each Insight references exactly one Standard or domain. The referenced standard corresponds to the proficiency data that triggered the recommendation.

businessRules.title: Feedback Immutability
businessRules.description: Once feedback is given (helpful or not_helpful), it cannot be changed. Feedback is stored per-insight per-user.

businessRules.title: Dismissal Behavior
businessRules.description: Dismissed Insights are hidden from the active panel but retained in the system for analytics. Dismissal is per-user and does not affect other teachers viewing the same class.

businessRules.title: Session Grouping
businessRules.description: Insights generated in the same navigation action share a Session ID. This allows the system to track which insights were co-generated.

businessRules.title: No Manual Creation
businessRules.description: Insights cannot be manually created by users. They are exclusively system-generated by the AI engine based on proficiency data.

businessRules.title: Regeneration
businessRules.description: Teachers can request regeneration, which creates a new session with fresh insights for the current domain. Previous session insights remain accessible but are superseded.

businessRules.title: Data Freshness
businessRules.description: Insights are generated from the most recent proficiency data available at generation time. They do not auto-update when new scores arrive; regeneration is required.

states.name: Generated
states.description: AI engine has produced the insight; not yet seen by a teacher
states.triggers: AI generation triggered by Skills Grid navigation

states.name: Viewed
states.description: A teacher has opened or scrolled to this insight
states.triggers: Teacher views the insight in the side panel

states.name: Dismissed
states.description: Teacher has dismissed this insight from their view
states.triggers: Teacher clicks dismiss; per-user, insight persists for analytics

relationships.mechanics: Insights reference Students identified by the AI engine as relevant to the proficiency pattern. The relationship is system-generated and read-only for users.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. An Insight can reference multiple Students; a Student can appear in multiple Insights across domains and sessions.
relationships.sorts: Referenced students within an Insight: alphabetical by last name (default), by proficiency level in the relevant domain.
relationships.filters: By proficiency level, by class membership, by whether the student has been acted on.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated or transferred, they are removed from future Insight generation but persist in historical Insights.

relationships.mechanics: Each Insight is scoped to a single Standard or domain. The Standard provides the proficiency context that the AI engine analyzed to produce the recommendation.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. An Insight references one Standard; a Standard can be referenced by many Insights across classes and sessions.
relationships.sorts: Insights referencing a Standard: by generation date (most recent first), by type.
relationships.filters: By insight type, by feedback status, by dismissed state.
relationships.dependencies: If a Standard is retired or restructured, associated Insights become historical and no new Insights are generated for that Standard.

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: Students referenced by this insight's AI recommendation

nestedObjects.name: Standard
nestedObjects.description: The standard or domain this insight pertains to

allAttributes.name: Insight ID
allAttributes.description: Unique system identifier for this insight instance.

allAttributes.name: Insight Type
allAttributes.description: Category of insight: classwide_learning_need or student_grouping_suggestion. Determines visual treatment and recommended actions.

allAttributes.name: Title
allAttributes.description: Concise headline summarizing the insight recommendation. AI-generated, not user-editable.

allAttributes.name: Body/Description
allAttributes.description: Full narrative explanation of the insight with supporting data and suggested actions.

allAttributes.name: Referenced Students
allAttributes.description: Students relevant to this insight recommendation.

allAttributes.name: Referenced Standard/Domain
allAttributes.description: The standard or domain this insight pertains to.

allAttributes.name: Feedback Status
allAttributes.description: Teacher feedback on insight quality: none, helpful, or not_helpful. Default: none.

allAttributes.name: Session ID
allAttributes.description: Links this insight to the AI generation session that produced it. Used for session-level analytics.

allAttributes.name: Generated Timestamp
allAttributes.description: When this insight was generated by the AI engine. Used for freshness and ordering.

allAttributes.name: Dismissed
allAttributes.description: Whether the teacher has dismissed this insight from view. Per-user state. Default: false.

structure.evidence: An Insight has structured attributes: title, body, insight type, referenced students, referenced standard, feedback status, session ID, timestamp, and dismissal state. You can design a detail view for an Insight.

instances.evidence: 'Classwide Learning Need: Number Sense' (18 students below proficient), 'Suggested Grouping: Geometry Foundations' (5 students with shared gaps), 'Classwide Learning Need: Reading Comprehension' (22 students across 3 classes). Each Skills Grid session generates multiple Insight instances.

purpose.evidence: Insights bridge the gap between raw proficiency data and instructional action. Teachers view Insights to identify patterns, form student groups, and decide on re-teaching strategies. The feedback loop improves AI recommendations over time.

sipValidation.verdict: Core AI-driven object. Translates proficiency data into actionable teacher recommendations within the Skills Grid.

synonyms.notes: Used interchangeably in early design docs; Insight is the canonical term
Sometimes used in UI text to soften the directive tone
Used by engineering teams; conflates with task-tracking action items
Technical term used in model documentation

objectViews.description: AI-generated insights shown as cards in a side panel alongside the skills grid. Teachers review instructional suggestions, give feedback, or dismiss ones that aren't useful.
A standalone insight card highlighting a student or standard pattern. Teachers scan these to catch actionable recommendations they might otherwise miss.
Insights listed in a data table with type, standard, and feedback status. Admins monitor which insights are being generated and how teachers are responding to them.
An insight shown as a compact header within the Star dashboard, surfacing a key recommendation tied to assessment data.
